fact: August 8 is exactly 220 days into the year - that's more than 7 months of Earth spinning around!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak cricket season and male crickets are chirping their loudest love songs to attract mates.
The sun rises around 6 AM and sets around 8 PM in most of North America, giving you 14 hours of daylight to play outside.
Leo season is in full swing! People born on August 8 are Leos, represented by the fierce lion.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, it's the middle of winter and some areas might even have snow on the ground.
Perseid meteor showers are ramping up around this time - you might see shooting stars if you look up at night!
Many flowers like sunflowers and black-eyed Susans are at their tallest and most colorful in North America right now.
The Earth is still moving away from the sun after our closest approach in early July, but it's so gradual you can't feel it.
In northern regions like Canada and Alaska, some animals are already starting to grow thicker fur for the coming winter.
August 8 falls during the hottest part of summer in most places, when asphalt can get hot enough to fry an egg!
There are only 145 days left in the year after August 8 - less than 5 months until New Year's Day!
In tropical rainforests near the equator, this date marks no seasonal change at all - it's just another hot, humid day like every other day of the year.
